patches.hypothesis: The bug occurs because the method incorrectly checks the trailer byte when all characters in the buffer are spaces or NULs, leading to an IllegalArgumentException being thrown instead of returning 0.

The original code incorrectly throws an exception when the trimmed buffer is empty after removing trailing spaces and NULs. This fix ensures that the method handles such cases gracefully without throwing an exception, while also ensuring that other edge cases like very large numbers and invalid characters are handled correctly.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the method incorrectly throwing an IllegalArgumentException when the buffer contains only trailing spaces or NULs after trimming. The check should allow for a valid octal number even if it is followed by spaces or NULs.

patches.hypothesis: The bug is caused by incorrect handling of the maximum octal value. The method should correctly parse the maximum octal value without throwing an exception.

The bug is caused by the incorrect handling of the trailing space or NUL character. The method incorrectly throws an exception when the buffer contains only spaces or NULs, which should be valid input returning 0L.
The bug is caused by the incorrect handling of the maximum octal value. The method should correctly parse the maximum 11-digit octal value without throwing an exception.

The original code does not correctly handle cases where the buffer contains the maximum valid octal value followed by a space or NUL. The issue lies in the trailing space/NUL trimming logic, which can incorrectly terminate parsing before reaching the full octal value. The refined fix ensures that the trailing space/NUL is correctly handled without prematurely ending the parsing process.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by incorrect handling of the buffer when it contains the maximum valid octal value followed by a space or NUL. The method should correctly parse the maximum octal value without throwing an exception.
